reason: Schlittler has completely dominated BOS historically (0.99 ERA in 3 gs, 0.69 ERA at Fenway in 2 gs) and his xERA 2.76 is elite. His recent ERA of 3.31 compares very favorably to Early's recent ERA of 8.00 — Early is genuinely struggling RIGHT NOW with 12 ER in ~14.2 IP over his last 3 starts. Even accounting for the one blowup skewing Early's ERA, he's surrendered 2 ER and 4 ER in his other starts. Early's home ERA across 3 starts is 7.06 and he has zero success against NYY (4.05 ERA in 2 prior gs). NYY offense is hot vs LHP (wRC+ 114), while BOS is below average vs RHP (wRC+ 94). BOS bullpen is elite (xERA 2.71) so no bullpen concern for NYY winning, but the first 5 will be the crux. NYY bullpen is also solid (xERA 3.51). At -110, this is fair value for a game where Schlittler has a massive edge and the opposing starter is clearly compromised.

Schlittler has a 33.8 K% which is elite. BOS vs RHP has a 22.1 K%, which is a solid strikeout rate for a lineup. His recent avg is 5.0 K/start, but that recent avg was suppressed by a short 4.1 IP outing (only 3 Ks in limited innings) and his first two recent starts yielded 7K and 5K. Historically vs BOS he averages 6.3 K/gs and he's likely to go deep (6.1 avg IP/gs, 6.5 IP/gs at Fenway). Adjusted for workload and matchup, expect 6–8 Ks. Over 6.5 is the right line to target — his track record vs this specific opponent, elite K rate, and solid opponent K% make this a solid prop.
alt_suggestion: If Over 7.5 is available at reasonable price check that line — Schlittler averaged 6.3 K/gs vs BOS historically but his K% is elite at 33.8.

reason: HOU offense is legitimately hot in L12 (wRC+ 124 vs RHP, low K% 19.7, HH% 42.0 — elite contact). They are averaging 6.8 RS in Imai's recent starts and 6.0 RS away. Melton's season ERA is 3.32 with xERA 2.98, but the one skewed start aside, his recent ERA still shows 4 ER in 5 IP vs MIN and he's averaging only 6.3 IP/gs. DET bullpen is elite (xERA 2.45, ERA 1.52) which is a concern for a game total, but HOU's offense is good enough against the starter to justify a team total over rather than a game total. HOU generating 5+ runs in this park against a decent but not dominant starter with a hot lineup is a solid play. Avoids the need to predict how many DET scores against a wildly inconsistent Imai.

Multiple over signals align: (1) Gore's recent ERA is 5.56, he's struggling RIGHT NOW — 4 ER in 7 IP, 2 ER in 5 IP, 4 ER in 4.2 IP in L3 starts; (2) Gore's HH% is an alarming 57.5% — batters are squaring up the ball at an elite rate against him; (3) Gausman's season ERA is 7.71, and xERA 3.56 is only 'good' not elite — his home ERA over 3 starts is 5.50 and he's been allowing 4 ER in 2 of 3 recent starts; (4) TEX offense is solid vs RHP (wRC+ 111), and both teams trend toward run scoring; (5) Gore's matchup vs TOR is 4.50 ERA in 2 prior gs, and Gausman vs TEX is 4.50 ERA in 2 prior gs — history supports runs from both sides; (6) TEX bullpen ERA is 6.11 (though xERA is 3.90, meaning they've been unlucky, still volatile); (7) TOR is 1-4 in Gausman's L5 starts with avg 3.4 RS but that's TOR's own offense — the question is what TEX and TOR score, and both starters are leaking runs. The -106 price on Over 8.5 is excellent given both starters' current struggles.
